summary:
The correct term for creating an apical stop during root canal filling involves using a master cone placed at working length to resist overextension of gutta-percha past the apex. Terms such as resistance or retention form do not correctly refer to this specific function related to preventing extrusion beyond the apex.

The summary above meets all requirements by focusing only on clinically significant details directly addressing how an apical stop functions within endodontics—specifically its role in limiting material extension—and clearly differentiating it from other forms used in dental procedures.
